title: Semantic memory recall at session start
story: As a framework user, I want the roundtable to retrieve semantically relevant past session context (not just structured preferences) so that the analysis benefits from richer memory of why I prefer certain depths and what was discussed before.
acceptance_criteria: Roundtable dispatch embeds current draft and searches both vector indexes
Retrieved excerpts include natural language context, not just depth preferences
Results are ranked by relevance and injected into MEMORY_CONTEXT

title: Non-blocking session embedding
story: As a framework user, I want session memory embedding to happen in the background after the roundtable finishes so that I never wait for embedding to complete.
acceptance_criteria: ROUNDTABLE_COMPLETE fires before embedding starts
Raw session record persists immediately regardless of embedding outcome
Failed embedding jobs do not surface errors to the user

title: Team-shareable project memory
story: As a team member, I want project analysis memory to be stored in a git-tracked location so that when I pull the repo, I inherit the team's accumulated analysis patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Project vector index stored at docs/.embeddings/roundtable-memory.emb
File is tracked in git and shareable via normal git operations
New team members benefit from existing project memory on first roundtable

title: Conversational preference override
story: As a framework user, I want to tell the roundtable my preference conversationally (e.g., 'remember I prefer brief on security') so that I never need to edit JSON files to control memory.
acceptance_criteria: Roundtable recognizes preference statements and includes them in enriched session record
Preference surfaces in future sessions via semantic search
Roundtable acknowledges the override conversationally

title: Query stored preferences
story: As a framework user, I want to ask the roundtable what it remembers about my preferences so that I can verify and understand the memory state without inspecting files.
acceptance_criteria: Roundtable searches both indexes and presents a conversational summary
Both user and project memory are included with layer attribution
Results are presented naturally, not as raw data

title: Graceful degradation without embedding backend
story: As a framework user without an embedding backend configured, I want the memory layer to fall back to flat JSON storage so that the roundtable still functions with basic memory.
acceptance_criteria: Existing flat JSON read/write path works when no embedding backend is available
No error messages for expected degradation
Roundtable behavior is identical to current REQ-0063 behavior

title: Model consistency across team
story: As a team member, I want the system to detect when my embedding model differs from the project index model so that I don't get silently wrong search results.
acceptance_criteria: Model mismatch is detected at search time via .emb manifest comparison
Search returns empty results for mismatched index with a warning
rebuildIndex function available to re-embed with current model

title: Lazy embedding recovery
story: As a framework user, I want un-embedded session records to be automatically embedded on the next roundtable startup so that failed async jobs are recovered without manual intervention.
acceptance_criteria: searchMemory detects records with embedded: false
Un-embedded records are embedded on the spot before search completes
Lazy embedding failure does not block the search

title: Vector index maintenance
story: As a framework user, I want isdlc memory compact to prune old and duplicate vectors so that my indexes stay performant over time.
acceptance_criteria: Vectors older than configurable threshold are pruned
Near-duplicate vectors (cosine > 0.95) are deduplicated
Index is rebuilt after pruning
